rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical dehydrogenative annulation of N-allyl amides with 1,3-dicarbonyl compounds to form pyrrolidines [(4+1)] or tetrahydropyridines [(4+2)] using a phenothiazine organic redox mediator
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of phenothiazine (3, E_p/2 = 0.52 V vs SCE) to its radical cation 3·+, which serves as an outer-sphere SET mediator that oxidizes the deprotonated 1,3-dicarbonyl anion to a C-radical; cathode reduces H2O to H2 and HO- (which deprotonates the 1,3-dicarbonyl)

key_intermediates.name: tertiary C-radical V after addition to N-allyl amide alkene

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes phenothiazine 3 to radical cation 3·+; cathode reduces H2O to H2 + HO-, which deprotonates the 1,3-dicarbonyl to enolate II; 3·+ oxidizes II by SET to C-radical III, which adds across the N-allyl amide alkene to give tertiary radical V; V is further oxidized by 3·+ to a cation that is intramolecularly trapped by the amide carbonyl oxygen, leading after workup to the pyrrolidine (via open-chain 62) or tetrahydropyridine product.

probe_reagents.name: crossover experiment with amides 56 and 57
probe_reagents.observation: Only non-crossed products 14 and 37 formed; no crossover products detected, indicating intramolecular acyl migration

probe_reagents.name: N-Boc analogue 58
probe_reagents.observation: Cyclic carbamate 59 isolated in 45% yield, supporting trapping of cationic intermediate by amide/carbamate carbonyl oxygen

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O
probe_reagents.observation: [18O]-11 obtained in 59% yield with 90% 18O incorporation; LiAlH4 reduction gave diol 61 with no 18O — confirms acyl oxygen of product originates from H2O

probe_reagents.name: isolation of open-chain intermediate 62
probe_reagents.observation: Run in H2O-free tBuOMe/MeOH gave uncyclized amine 62 (53%); resubjection to standard conditions cyclized to pyrrolidine 4 (42%), confirming 62 as on-cycle intermediate

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 2, 3, and N-allyl amide IV
probe_reagents.observation: E_p/2: 3 = 0.52 V, anion II = 0.31 V, dicarbonyl 2 > 2.0 V, alkene IV = 1.74 V (vs SCE); mediator selectively oxidizes the enolate

notes: Alternative cyclization onto the 1,3-dicarbonyl carbonyl oxygen (instead of amide carbonyl) was considered but ruled out by the 18O-labelling result and the Boc-trapping experiment, which show the product acyl-O derives from water via amide-O attack on cation VI, followed by hydrolysis. Phenothiazine is highlighted as a novel organic redox catalyst operative in aqueous solution, avoiding stoichiometric oxidants and transition metals.
